poster_title: Multi-Agent-as-Judge: Aligning LLM-Agent-Based Automated Evaluation with Multi-Dimensional Human Evaluation
authors: Jiaju Chen, Yuxuan Lu, Xiaojie Wang, Huimin Zeng, Jing Huang, Jiri Gesi, Ying Xu, Bingsheng Yao, Dakuo Wang

content: Collaborative human work requires multi-dimensional evaluations.
Human expert annotation is costly and labor-intensive.
Current LLM-as-a-judge approaches lack systematic design.
Task-specific evaluation setups are not easily adaptable.
MAJ-EVAL aims to provide a generalizable solution.
Lack of systematic methodology for designing agent personas.
Non-generalizable evaluation setups for new domains or scenarios.
Need for robust and scalable evaluation methods.
Alignment with human ratings is crucial for NLG evaluation.
MAJ-EVAL addresses these challenges.
Extract diverse perspectives from research papers.
Construct corresponding personas for LLM agents.
Agents engage in in-group debates to refine judgments.
Aggregator agent synthesizes ratings from all groups.
Evaluated on QAG for children’s storybook reading.
Evaluated on multi-document summarization of medical literature.
Compared with traditional automated metrics and existing methods.
Achieved better alignment with human ratings in both domains.
Demonstrated potential as a generalizable solution.
panels.figure_caption: Spearman’s ρ (rank-based correlation) between evaluation methods and human ratings on StorySparkQA.

content: MAJ-EVAL outperformed traditional metrics and existing methods.
The framework is effective across different domains and tasks.
Agent personas and debate process enhance evaluation robustness.
Initial and final scores show significant improvement.
Scalable and consistent multi-dimensional evaluation is possible.
MAJ-EVAL provides a systematic and generalizable framework.
The method shows promise for real-world NLG applications.
Further fine-tuning may be required for specific tasks.
Quality of personas depends on input text documents.
Future work includes expanding to more domains and tasks.

Fig7.caption: MAJ-EVAL’s multi-agent-as-judge debate evaluation process (Step 2). Each agent first independently
Fig7.description: 描述 MAJ-EVAL 的多代理评估过程的第二步，每个代理首先独立评估。

Fig15.caption: Spearman’s ρ (rank-based correlation) between evaluation methods and human ratings on StorySparkQA
Fig15.description: 展示不同评估方法与人类评分在 StorySparkQA 数据集上的斯皮尔曼等级相关性。
